verses.text: A great crowd has nothing to eat; 'I have compassion on the multitude,' lest they faint. Pitying Lord. He feels the bodily hunger of the people.
A great crowd has nothing to eat; 'I have compassion on the multitude,' lest they faint. Pitying Lord. He feels the bodily hunger of the people.
A great crowd has nothing to eat; 'I have compassion on the multitude,' lest they faint. Pitying Lord. He feels the bodily hunger of the people.
With seven loaves He prepares to feed them, having them sit down. Renewed power. The Saviour will not send the faint away unfed.
With seven loaves He prepares to feed them, having them sit down. Renewed power. The Saviour will not send the faint away unfed.
With seven loaves He prepares to feed them, having them sit down. Renewed power. The Saviour will not send the faint away unfed.
He blesses the few fishes too, and four thousand are filled, seven baskets over. Abundant supply. His store is never spent by the need before Him.
He blesses the few fishes too, and four thousand are filled, seven baskets over. Abundant supply. His store is never spent by the need before Him.
He blesses the few fishes too, and four thousand are filled, seven baskets over. Abundant supply. His store is never spent by the need before Him.
The Pharisees seek a sign; He sighs deeply, 'There shall no sign be given.' Grieved refusal. Hardened unbelief is not won by more wonders.
The Pharisees seek a sign; He sighs deeply, 'There shall no sign be given.' Grieved refusal. Hardened unbelief is not won by more wonders.
The Pharisees seek a sign; He sighs deeply, 'There shall no sign be given.' Grieved refusal. Hardened unbelief is not won by more wonders.
The Pharisees seek a sign; He sighs deeply, 'There shall no sign be given.' Grieved refusal. Hardened unbelief is not won by more wonders.
He warns of 'the leaven of the Pharisees'; the disciples think He means bread. Slow to grasp. They mistake the spiritual for the literal.
He warns of 'the leaven of the Pharisees'; the disciples think He means bread. Slow to grasp. They mistake the spiritual for the literal.

'Having eyes, see ye not?' He recalls the loaves and baskets; 'how is it that ye do not understand?' Dull hearts. They forget the very miracles they saw.
'Having eyes, see ye not?' He recalls the loaves and baskets; 'how is it that ye do not understand?' Dull hearts. They forget the very miracles they saw.

At Bethsaida a blind man first sees 'men as trees,' then clearly after a second touch. Gradual sight. Christ's work in us is sometimes by stages.

'Whom say ye that I am?' Peter answers, 'Thou art the Christ.' The confession. Here is the truth on which all faith rests.
'Whom say ye that I am?' Peter answers, 'Thou art the Christ.' The confession. Here is the truth on which all faith rests.
'Whom say ye that I am?' Peter answers, 'Thou art the Christ.' The confession. Here is the truth on which all faith rests.
He foretells His death; Peter rebukes Him, and hears, 'Get thee behind me, Satan.' Mistaken love. Even good intent opposes God when it shuns the cross.

'Whosoever will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross'; what shall a man give for his soul? The cost. To follow Christ is to lose self and gain the soul.
